title: sled-storage/db: Define Database struct and tree constants
description: Define tree name constants and Database struct wrapping sled::Db with tree handle fields for all 7 trees (instances, journal, timers, signals, run_queue, activities, workflows).

resolved: Tree handles stored as struct fields, not accessed via open_tree each time
Database owns the sled::Db and all tree references
Error type uses thiserror for derive(Debug) derives
assumptions: sled::Db is Send + Sync, allowing Database to be Send + Sync
Trees are cheap to clone (sled::Tree is Arc-based internally)

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL define all 7 tree name constants as byte slices

postconditions: Database struct fields are initialized with proper tree handles
invariants: Tree constants are valid UTF-8 byte strings

happy: Database struct can be instantiated with all tree fields

phase_0: Read ADR-007 for tree name specifications
phase_1: Write unit tests for Database struct instantiation
phase_2: Define 7 const tree name byte slices
Define Database struct with tree handle fields

title: sled-storage/db: Implement open_db function
description: Implement open_db(path) that opens or creates a sled database at the given path and opens all 7 trees (instances, journal, timers, signals, run_queue, activities, workflows). Returns Database wrapping the opened sled::Db.

resolved: open_db creates trees if they don't exist via open_tree
assumptions: Path is a valid directory path writable by the process

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL open existing database if path contains one
THE SYSTEM SHALL create new database if path is empty
unwanted: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic if database already exists

preconditions: path must be a valid directory path
postconditions: Database is returned with all 7 trees opened
All trees are accessible via struct fields

happy: open_db creates database in temp directory
error: open_db returns error for invalid path

phase_1: Write tests for open_db with temp directory
Write test that opens same database twice
phase_2: Return Database struct with all tree handles

title: sled-storage/db: Implement close_db function
description: Implement close_db() that performs graceful shutdown of the database. Calls flush before closing to ensure all data is persisted.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL flush all trees before closing
unwanted: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT leave data unflushed on close

preconditions: Database must not have already been closed
postconditions: All data is flushed to disk

phase_2: Implement close_db that calls db.flush()?; to ensure data is written

title: sled-storage/db: Implement get_tree function
description: Implement get_tree(name) that retrieves a tree by name. Returns a cloned tree handle for the given tree name. Returns Err(DbError) if tree name is unknown.

resolved: Tree names match the 7 defined constants
assumptions: Unknown tree names return error, not None

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return tree handle for valid tree name
unwanted: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return tree for unknown tree name

preconditions: name must be one of the 7 defined tree names

error: get_tree returns error for unknown tree name

phase_1: Write tests for get_tree with valid and invalid names
phase_2: Implement get_tree using match on tree name bytes
Match against INSTANCES, JOURNAL, TIMERS, SIGNALS, RUN_QUEUE, ACTIVITIES, WORKFLOWS

title: sled-storage/db: Implement flush_all function
description: Implement flush_all() that flushes all 7 trees to disk using flush_async. Returns Result<(), DbError>.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL flush all trees on flush_all call

postconditions: All 7 trees are flushed to disk

phase_1: Write test that writes data then flush_all
phase_2: Implement flush_all that calls tree.flush_async()?; for each tree

title: sled-storage/db: Add integration tests
description: Add comprehensive integration tests for the db module covering open_db, close_db, get_tree, and flush_all with temp directory handling.

resolved: Tests use temp directories for isolation

happy: Full workflow: open -> insert -> flush -> close -> reopen
edge: Multiple databases in different temp dirs

phase_2: Test database lifecycle: create, write, flush, close, reopen
Test get_tree with all 7 tree names
